steps.title: Convert the known biking time
steps.content:
One hour of bicycling is two $30$-minute periods. The bicycling calories are
$$180\cdot 2=360$$

steps.title: Find the remaining calories
steps.content:
Subtract the calories already used from the total.
$$2{,}760-360=2{,}400$$

steps.title: Solve for hiking hours
steps.content:
Hiking uses $240$ calories per $30$ minutes, so let $h$ be the number of $30$-minute hiking periods.
$$240h=2{,}400$$
$$h=10$$
Ten $30$-minute periods is $5$ hours. The answer is D.
